TEXT.citation: Wikipedia contributors. (2026). Sakizaya Wikipedia. Wikimedia Foundation. Retrieved 2026-06-09, from https://szy.wikipedia.org/
TEXT.BibTeX_citation: @misc{Wiki_szy, title = {Sakizaya Wikipedia}, author = {{Wikipedia contributors}}, year = {2026}, publisher = {Wikimedia Foundation}, url = {https://szy.wikipedia.org/}, urldate = {2026-06-09} }

S.FORM: == bali == Á©ļśį£ u sakauzip ita ku bali. ťĘ® u bali han ku pasaungayay tu pahpah nu kilang. siwkay nu cait bali1- anu inayi' ku bali sa, amapatay ku tademaw. la'cus pahanhan tu kita. bali2- anu sibali sa, saepi' tu kita. yu adidi' ku bali, kapah. anu tabaki ku bali, u balius kuyza, malalud, maelul ku niyazu' atu tademaw. tinaku a kamu pabali śČďśį£ÔľõŚÖÖśį£ a:su' ku heci nu apalu pahipuwan. amabaliyus ťĘĪťĘ®ŚįáŤá≥ amabaliyus tu sa pihaymawi kita. baliyus ťĘĪťĘ® u papah nu salunan ku sakatineng tu baliyus. hinabaliyus ŚłłśúČťĘĪťĘ® hinabaliyus i lalud. kabaliyusan ťĘĪťĘ®Ś≠£ÁĮÄ u kabaliyusan a puu' nu mihecaan sa u lalud. mabali śúČťĘ® mabali kina demiad. amana pisiwduk a mabali. mapabali ŤĘęťĘ®ŚźĻ la'cus mapabali ku makebingay. pabalien ŚéĽśČďśį£ÔľõŚéĽŚÖÖśį£ pabalien ni Sayun ku mali ni Panay! sibali śúČťĘ® sibali ku luma' ni Tilu tu labi. sibaliay śúČťĘ®ÁöĄ malecad u sibaliay ku bili ni Taymu tanuesus sa. u sulit nu Hulam atu Amilika . The three major constituents of air, and therefore of Earth's atmosphere, are nitrogen, oxygen, and argon. Water vapor accounts for roughly 0.25% of the atmosphere by mass. The concentration of water vapor varies significantly from around 10 ppm by volume in the coldest portions of the atmosphere to as much as 5% by volume in hot, humid air masses, and concentrations of other atmospheric gases are typically quoted in terms of dry air . The remaining gases are often referred to as trace gases, among which are the greenhouse gases, principally carbon dioxide, methane, nitrous oxide, and ozone. Filtered air includes trace amounts of many other chemical compounds. Many substances of natural origin may be present in locally and seasonally variable small amounts as aerosols in an unfiltered air sample, including dust of mineral and organic composition, pollen and spores, sea spray, and volcanic ash. Various industrial pollutants also may be present as gases or aerosols, such as chlorine , fluorine compounds and elemental mercury vapor. Sulfur compounds such as hydrogen sulfide and sulfur dioxide (SO2) may be derived from natural sources or from industrial air pollution. Wind is the flow of gases on a large scale. On the surface of the Earth, wind consists of the bulk movement of air. In outer space, solar wind is the movement of gases or charged particles from the Sun through space, while planetary wind is the outgassing of light chemical elements from a planet's atmosphere into space. Winds are commonly classified by their spatial scale, their speed, the types of forces that cause them, the regions in which they occur, and their effect. The strongest observed winds on a planet in the Solar System occur on Neptune and Saturn. Winds have various aspects, an important one being its velocity ; another the density of the gas involved; another its energy content or wind energy.
S.PHON: == bar~…ĺi == ** u sakauzip ita ku bar~…ĺi. * u bar~…ĺi ńßan ku pasauŇčajaj tu pańßpańß nu kir~…ĺaŇč. siwkaj nu ¶ait bar~…ĺi*- anu inaji Ē~ ° ku bar~…ĺi sa, amapataj ku tad…ômaw. r~…ĺa Ē~ ° ¶us pańßanńßan tu kita. bar~…ĺi*- anu sibar~…ĺi sa, sa…ôpi Ē~ ° tu kita. ju adidi Ē~ ° ku bar~…ĺi, kapańß. anu tabaki ku bar~…ĺi, u bar~…ĺius kujza, mar~…ĺar~…ĺud, ma…ôr~…ĺur~…ĺ ku nijazu Ē~ ° atu tad…ômaw. tinaku a kamu pabar~…ĺi ***** a:su Ē~ ° ku ńß…ô ¶i nu apar~…ĺu pańßipuwan. amabar~…ĺijus **** amabar~…ĺijus tu sa pińßajmawi kita. bar~…ĺijus ** u papańß nu sar~…ĺunan ku sakatin…ôŇč tu bar~…ĺijus. ńßinabar~…ĺijus **** ńßinabar~…ĺijus i r~…ĺar~…ĺud. kabar~…ĺijusan **** u kabar~…ĺijusan a puu Ē~ ° nu mińß…ô ¶aan sa u r~…ĺar~…ĺud. mabar~…ĺi ** mabar~…ĺi kina d…ômiad. amana pisiwduk a mabar~…ĺi. mapabar~…ĺi *** r~…ĺa Ē~ ° ¶us mapabar~…ĺi ku mak…ôbiŇčaj. pabar~…ĺi…ôn ******* pabar~…ĺi…ôn ni sajun ku mar~…ĺi ni panaj! sibar~…ĺi ** sibar~…ĺi ku r~…ĺuma Ē~ ° ni tir~…ĺu tu r~…ĺabi. sibar~…ĺiaj *** mar~…ĺ…ô ¶ad u sibar~…ĺiaj ku bir~…ĺi ni tajmu tanu…ôsus sa. u sur~…ĺit nu ńßur~…ĺam atu amir~…ĺika . tńß…ô tńßr…ô…ô major ¶onstitu…ônts of air, and tńß…ôr…ôfor…ô of …ôartńß Ē~ °s atmospńß…ôr…ô, ar…ô nitrog…ôn, o*jg…ôn, and argon. wat…ôr *apor a ¶ ¶ounts for rougńßr~…ĺj *.**% of tńß…ô atmospńß…ôr…ô bj mass. tńß…ô ¶on ¶…ôntration of wat…ôr *apor *ari…ôs signifi ¶antr~…ĺj from around ** ppm bj *or~…ĺum…ô in tńß…ô ¶or~…ĺd…ôst portions of tńß…ô atmospńß…ôr…ô to as mu ¶ńß as *% bj *or~…ĺum…ô in ńßot, ńßumid air mass…ôs, and ¶on ¶…ôntrations of otńß…ôr atmospńß…ôri ¶ gas…ôs ar…ô tjpi ¶ar~…ĺr~…ĺj *uot…ôd in t…ôrms of drj air . tńß…ô r…ômainiŇč gas…ôs ar…ô oft…ôn r…ôf…ôrr…ôd to as tra ¶…ô gas…ôs, amoŇč wńßi ¶ńß ar…ô tńß…ô gr…ô…ônńßous…ô gas…ôs, prin ¶ipar~…ĺr~…ĺj ¶arbon dio*id…ô, m…ôtńßan…ô, nitrous o*id…ô, and ozon…ô. fir~…ĺt…ôr…ôd air in ¶r~…ĺud…ôs tra ¶…ô amounts of manj otńß…ôr ¶ńß…ômi ¶ar~…ĺ ¶ompounds. manj substan ¶…ôs of naturar~…ĺ origin maj b…ô pr…ôs…ônt in and spor…ôs, s…ôa spraj, and *or~…ĺ ¶ani ¶ asńß. *arious industriar~…ĺ por~…ĺr~…ĺutants ar~…ĺso maj b…ô pr…ôs…ônt as gas…ôs or a…ôrosor~…ĺs, su ¶ńß as ¶ńßr~…ĺorin…ô , fr~…ĺuorin…ô ¶ompounds and por~…ĺr~…ĺution. wind is tńß…ô fr~…ĺow of gas…ôs on a r~…ĺarg…ô s ¶ar~…ĺ…ô. on tńß…ô surfa ¶…ô of tńß…ô …ôartńß, wind ¶onsists of tńß…ô bur~…ĺk mo*…ôm…ônt of air. in out…ôr spa ¶…ô, sor~…ĺar wind is tńß…ô mo*…ôm…ônt of gas…ôs or ¶ńßarg…ôd parti ¶r~…ĺ…ôs from tńß…ô sun tńßrougńß spa ¶…ô, wńßir~…ĺ…ô pr~…ĺan…ôtarj wind is tńß…ô outgassiŇč of r~…ĺigńßt ¶ńß…ômi ¶ar~…ĺ …ôr~…ĺ…ôm…ônts from a pr~…ĺan…ôt Ē~ °s atmospńß…ôr…ô into spa ¶…ô. winds ar…ô ¶ommonr~…ĺj ¶r~…ĺassifi…ôd bj tńß…ôir spatiar~…ĺ s ¶ar~…ĺ…ô, tńß…ôir sp…ô…ôd, tńß…ô tjp…ôs of for ¶…ôs tńßat ¶aus…ô tńß…ôm, tńß…ô r…ôgions in wńßi ¶ńß tńß…ô o ¶ ¶ur, and tńß…ôir …ôff…ô ¶t. tńß…ô stroŇč…ôst obs…ôr*…ôd winds on a pr~…ĺan…ôt in tńß…ô sor~…ĺar sjst…ôm o ¶ ¶ur on n…ôptun…ô and saturn. winds ńßa*…ô *arious asp…ô ¶ts, an important on…ô b…ôiŇč its *…ôr~…ĺo ¶itj ; anotńß…ôr tńß…ô d…ônsitj of tńß…ô gas in*or~…ĺ*…ôd; anotńß…ôr its …ôn…ôrgj ¶ont…ônt or wind …ôn…ôrgj.
S.FORM: == bali == Á©ļśį£ u sakauzip ita ku bali. ťĘ® u bali han ku pasaungayay tu pahpah nu kilang. siwkay nu cait bali1- anu inayi' ku bali sa, amapatay ku tademaw. la'cus pahanhan tu kita. bali2- anu sibali sa, saepi' tu kita. yu adidi' ku bali, kapah. anu tabaki ku bali, u balius kuyza, malalud, maelul ku niyazu' atu tademaw. tinaku a kamu pabali śČďśį£ÔľõŚÖÖśį£ a:su' ku heci nu apalu pahipuwan. amabaliyus ťĘĪťĘ®ŚįáŤá≥ amabaliyus tu sa pihaymawi kita. baliyus ťĘĪťĘ® u papah nu salunan ku sakatineng tu baliyus. hinabaliyus ŚłłśúČťĘĪťĘ® hinabaliyus i lalud. kabaliyusan ťĘĪťĘ®Ś≠£ÁĮÄ u kabaliyusan a puu' nu mihecaan sa u lalud. mabali śúČťĘ® mabali kina demiad. amana pisiwduk a mabali. mapabali ŤĘęťĘ®ŚźĻ la'cus mapabali ku makebingay. pabalien ŚéĽśČďśį£ÔľõŚéĽŚÖÖśį£ pabalien ni Sayun ku mali ni Panay! sibali śúČťĘ® sibali ku luma' ni Tilu tu labi. sibaliay śúČťĘ®ÁöĄ malecad u sibaliay ku bili ni Taymu tanuesus sa. u sulit nu Hulam atu Amilika . The three major constituents of air, and therefore of Earth's atmosphere, are nitrogen, oxygen, and argon. Water vapor accounts for roughly 0.25% of the atmosphere by mass. The concentration of water vapor varies significantly from around 10 ppm by volume in the coldest portions of the atmosphere to as much as 5% by volume in hot, humid air masses, and concentrations of other atmospheric gases are typically quoted in terms of dry air . The remaining gases are often referred to as trace gases, among which are the greenhouse gases, principally carbon dioxide, methane, nitrous oxide, and ozone. Filtered air includes trace amounts of many other chemical compounds. Many substances of natural origin may be present in locally and seasonally variable small amounts as aerosols in an unfiltered air sample, including dust of mineral and organic composition, pollen and spores, sea spray, and volcanic ash. Various industrial pollutants also may be present as gases or aerosols, such as chlorine , fluorine compounds and elemental mercury vapor. Sulfur compounds such as hydrogen sulfide and sulfur dioxide (SO2) may be derived from natural sources or from industrial air pollution. Wind is the flow of gases on a large scale. On the surface of the Earth, wind consists of the bulk movement of air. In outer space, solar wind is the movement of gases or charged particles from the Sun through space, while planetary wind is the outgassing of light chemical elements from a planet's atmosphere into space. Winds are commonly classified by their spatial scale, their speed, the types of forces that cause them, the regions in which they occur, and their effect. The strongest observed winds on a planet in the Solar System occur on Neptune and Saturn. Winds have various aspects, an important one being its velocity ; another the density of the gas involved; another its energy content or wind energy.
